rxn_class_summary: Manganese-catalyzed electrochemical oxidative azidation-annulation cascade of N-arylacrylamides and N-arylcinnamamides with NaN3 to give azidated oxindoles and quinolinones.
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of a Mn(II)-N3 complex to a high-valent Mn(III)-N3 species which transfers azide to the alkene; the resulting cyclohexadienyl radical is further anodically oxidized to a cation that rearomatizes; H2 is evolved at the Pt cathode.

key_intermediates.name: tertiary C-radical from azide addition to acrylamide (C)
benzylic/resonance-stabilized radical from azide addition to cinnamamide (C')
cyclohexadienyl radical after ipso radical annulation onto N-aryl ring (D/D')
Wheland-type cation after anodic SET oxidation (E/E')

electron_flow: NaN3 binds Mn(II) to give Mn(II)-N3 (A), which is anodically oxidized by one electron to Mn(III)-N3 (B); B delivers an azidyl group to the alkene of the N-arylacrylamide/cinnamamide producing a C-centered radical (C/C'), which undergoes intramolecular radical annulation onto the N-aryl arene (D/D'); a second anodic SET gives cationic E/E', and deprotonative rearomatization yields the azidated oxindole or quinolinone, while protons are reduced to H2 at the Pt cathode.

probe_reagents.name: N-arylcinnamamide / N-arylacrylamide (substrate)
probe_reagents.observation: CV shows oxidation up to ~2.5 V (1.87 V peak), substrate not directly oxidized under operating conditions.

probe_reagents.name: NaN3
probe_reagents.observation: CV shows oxidation peak at 1.03 V.

probe_reagents.name: MnBr2
probe_reagents.observation: Two quasi-reversible oxidation peaks at 0.75 and 0.86 V assigned to Mn(II)/Mn(III) and free bromide oxidation.

probe_reagents.name: NaN3 + MnBr2 mixture
probe_reagents.observation: Azide oxidation peak shifts to 0.43 V, consistent with formation of Mn(II)-N3 / Mn(III)-N3 adduct that is more easily oxidized.

notes: An 'additional pathway' is depicted in Scheme 5 in which free azidyl radical (generated by direct anodic oxidation of N3-) adds to the alkene, offering an alternative to the favored Mn(III)-N3 inner-sphere azide transfer. Regioselectivity is rationalized by stability of the resulting radical (3° tertiary radical C for acrylamides, benzylic radical C' for cinnamamides). No radical trapping (TEMPO/BHT), KIE, or DFT experiments are reported in the shown pages.
